step_by_step_reasoning: We identify nodes chronologically from the whiteboard and transcript: User (UserConsumerWebMobile) initiates the process; API server (EC2) handles initial requests and generates S3 pre-signed URLs; S3 stores source and target images; Main SQS queues incoming requests; a Lambda router dynamically distributes tasks to GPU instances (EC2) across regions; a second SQS queue manages completion notifications; State Manager (EC2) processes these status notifications to update MySQL (ThirdParty); and CloudFront serves final video files to the user.

graph.name: MyHeritage - Scaling Deep Nostalgia with Multi-Region Spot Instances
graph.notes: Handles over 50M animations in two weeks. Implements multi-region GPU auto-scaling with G5 spot instances, managed via an intelligent Lambda router to optimize cost and avoid cross-region data transfer fees.

nodes.name: User
nodes.notes: Uploads photos and views animated results.

nodes.name: API Server
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles initial request, generates pre-signed S3 URLs, and handles status polling.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores raw source images and completed video artifacts.

nodes.name: Routing Lambda
nodes.notes: Dynamically routes animation tasks to different regions based on Spot capacity.

nodes.name: GPU Fleet
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Spot/On-Demand ASG running G5/P3 GPU instances processing deep-learning model.

nodes.name: State Manager
nodes.notes: Consumes completed events to update database.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Serves results globally using signed URLs.

edges.notes: Uploads source image directly to S3.
Downloads video via signed CloudFront URL.
